structural_claim: Continuous media spontaneously organize into discrete structures through symmetry-breaking instabilities where nonlinear interactions create preferred configurations

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Rotating flows undergo hierarchy of symmetry-breaking instabilities that spontaneously generate ordered structures
Thermal convection self-organizes into cellular patterns when driving forces overcome damping
High-pressure gradients in curved magnetic fields drive instabilities localized to bad curvature regions
Tokamak plasmas become unstable when safety factor q < 1, showing discrete stability boundaries
